# terrible_dellemc_unity_snapshotschedule (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `state` (String) Define whether the snapshot schedule should exist or not.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_delete` (Boolean) Indicates whether the system can automatically delete the snapshot.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `day_interval` (Number) Number of days between snapshots. Applicable only when rule type is `every_n_days`.
- `day_of_month` (Number) Day of the month for which the snapshot schedule rule applies. Applicable only when rule type is `every_month`. Value should be [1, 31].
- `days_of_week` (String) Days of the week for which the snapshot schedule rule applies. Applicable only when rule type is `every_week`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `desired_retention` (Number) The number of days/hours for which snapshot will be retained. When *auto_delete* is `true`, *desired_retention* cannot be specified. Maximum desired retention supported is 31 days or 744 hours.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `hour` (Number) The hour when the snapshot will be taken. Applicable for `every_n_days`, `every_week`, `every_month` rule types. For create operation, if *hour* parameter is not specified, value will be taken as `0`. Value should be [0, 23].
- `hours_of_day` (String) Hours of the day when the snapshot will be taken. Applicable only when rule type is `every_day`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interval` (Number) Number of hours between snapshots. Applicable only when rule type is `every_n_hours`.
- `minute` (Number) Minute offset from the hour when the snapshot will be taken. Applicable for all rule types. For a create operation, if *minute* parameter is not specified, value will be taken as `0`. Value should be [0, 59].
- `name` (String) The name of the snapshot schedule. Name is mandatory for a create operation. Specify either *name* or *id* (but not both) for any operation.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `retention_unit` (String) The retention unit for the snapshot.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) Type of the rule to be included in snapshot schedule. Type is mandatory for any create or modify operation. Once the snapshot schedule is created with one type it can be modified.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `snapshot_schedule_details` (String) Details of the snapshot schedule.
